page: Vultr vx1-m-8c-64gb is a memory-optimized virtual server belonging to the VX1 Cloud Compute family. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an AMD EPYC-Turin processor running at 2.0 GHz. The configuration includes 8 virtual CPUs (8 physical cores with 1.0 thread per core) on a shared CPU allocation model. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Local storage is limited to a 1 GB network-attached volume, and no dedicated GPU hardware is included.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-core capabilities alongside moderate multi-core performance. The server achieves top-tier status in single-core CPU performance via stress-ng (3616.08) and single-threaded gzip compression (39.67 GB/sec). However, multi-core stress-ng performance (14449.41) and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression (69.04 GB/sec) fall into the average tier, likely influenced by the shared CPU allocation. Memory bandwidth is stable, with read-only large block speeds at 72.42 GB/sec and write speeds at 41.4 GB/sec.

With its high memory capacity relative to processing cores, this server is well-suited for memory-bound workloads such as caching layers, database operations, and lightweight text processing. The shared CPU allocation represents a tradeoff, making it highly cost-efficient for workloads that experience intermittent processing spikes rather than sustained, intensive multi-core execution. It is appropriate for Redis deployments, static web serving, and small-scale language model inference, but less optimal for heavy multi-threaded compute tasks or large-scale local storage needs.
description: Vultr vx1-m-8c-64gb is a memory-optimized virtual server from the VX1 Cloud Compute family, featuring an x86_64 AMD EPYC-Turin processor running at 2.0 GHz. It provides 8 vCPUs on a shared CPU allocation model and 64.0 GB of system memory, establishing a high 8.0 GB per core ratio. While it lacks a dedicated GPU and offers only 1 GB of network-attached local storage, it delivers top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance. Multi-core and memory bandwidth benchmarks remain in the average tier. This resource profile makes the server highly cost-efficient for memory-intensive, non-sustained workloads. It is best suited for database operations, caching systems like Redis, static web serving, and lightweight text or language model processing.
og_description: Vultr vx1-m-8c-64gb is an x86_64 memory-optimized server with 8 AMD EPYC-Turin vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 1 GB network storage, delivering top-tier single-core performance for memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Vultr vx1-m-8c-64gb is an x86_64 memory-optimized server featuring 8 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and top-tier single-core performance.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring high RAM density and strong single-core performance for memory-bound workloads and caching.

High memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU
Top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance
Shared CPU allocation offering qualitative cost efficiency for variable workloads
Best suited for database operations, Redis caching, and static web serving
